keyInfo.info: Merge rate real do OpenClaw no dataset enriquecido é 24.1% (39% era artefato de amostra pequena).
keyInfo.context: Amostra expandida (1521 PRs enriquecidas) corrigiu viés da amostra inicial de 500 PRs.

keyInfo.info: Comentário de steipete é o sinal observável mais forte: ~74.5% merge quando presente (17x odds vs baseline ~13.3%).
keyInfo.context: Feature útil no modelo mature; não usar no early model porque aparece tarde no ciclo da PR.

keyInfo.info: Há forte aceleração temporal no processo de merge (ex.: W05=6.7% vs W08=54.3%).
keyInfo.context: weeks_since_open e/ou weekly_pr_volume são variáveis obrigatórias para reduzir confounding temporal.

keyInfo.info: Stage 0 (historical ingest + enrichment) está completo para janela nov/2025→fev/2026: 3233 PRs históricas, 1521 enriquecidas (47% coverage).
Label maintainer = 90.7% merge rate (183 PRs). Strongest label signal. Labels trusted-contributor (96) e experienced-contributor (99) também existem.
keyInfo.context: Features repo-específicas pro modelo early. Extraíveis deterministicamente.

keyInfo.info: Bootstrap v3 R1 accuracy (81%) já supera o melhor round do v2.1 inteiro (74.6% média). Salto atribuído a: dados enriquecidos (comments/reviews/files), Sonnet per-round patterns com 4 campos, anti-pattern field.
keyInfo.context: v2.1 R1 era 70% acc / 0.464 F1. v3 R1: 81% / 0.708. Múltiplas variáveis mudaram — não é possível isolar contribuição individual.

keyInfo.info: FP caiu de 18 (R1) para 6 (R4) — patterns de Sonnet efetivamente ensinam Haiku a evitar falsos positivos. Principal driver: patterns sobre first-time contributors sem maintainer engagement.
keyInfo.context: FP era o problema dominante do v2.1. Redução de 18→6 em 4 rounds confirma que pattern injection funciona.

keyInfo.title: Signal Inversion Bug — Sonnet consolidator reinforces error mechanisms instead of correcting them
keyInfo.description: P-1-1, P-1-2, P-1-9, P-1-11 prescribe the exact behavior that caused the FP errors they were derived from. Sonnet reads Haiku reasoning 'I over-trusted Greptile 5/5' and extracts 'Greptile 5/5 is a strong merge signal'. The consolidator confuses 'mechanism of the error' with 'rule to follow'. 4/13 patterns correct (31%), rest are noise or actively inverted signals. R2 FP increased 20→22, confirming patterns amplify rather than correct errors.
keyInfo.info: Signal Inversion Bug — Sonnet consolidator reinforces error mechanisms instead of correcting them. P-1-1, P-1-2, P-1-9, P-1-11 prescribe the exact behavior that caused the FP errors they were derived from. Sonnet reads Haiku reasoning 'I over-trusted Greptile 5/5' and extracts 'Greptile 5/5 is a strong merge signal'. The consolidator confuses 'mechanism of the error' with 'rule to follow'. 4/13 patterns correct (31%), rest are noise or actively inverted signals. R2 FP increased 20→22, confirming patterns amplify rather than correct errors.

keyInfo.title: v3 R3 = peak performance WITHOUT patterns — patterns degrade, not improve
keyInfo.description: v3 R3 (91% acc, F1 0.830) was the best round, achieved with 0 patterns. R4+ with patterns: accuracy dropped, FN exploded (2→12). v3 F1-learning (0.732) < F1-baseline (0.739). The bootstrap loop produces patterns that degrade performance relative to baseline. Combined with K033 (signal inversion), this explains WHY: patterns actively prescribe error-causing behavior.
keyInfo.info: v3 R3 = peak performance WITHOUT patterns — patterns degrade, not improve. v3 R3 (91% acc, F1 0.830) was the best round, achieved with 0 patterns. R4+ with patterns: accuracy dropped, FN exploded (2→12). v3 F1-learning (0.732) < F1-baseline (0.739). The bootstrap loop produces patterns that degrade performance relative to baseline. Combined with K033 (signal inversion), this explains WHY: patterns actively prescribe error-causing behavior.

keyInfo.title: Reflection vs Reasoning field — Sonnet was reading the wrong field
keyInfo.description: bootstrap_v4.py has a two-step process: (1) Haiku predicts with 'reasoning', (2) Haiku reflects on errors generating 'reflection'. The reflection is self-critique ('I missed X because Y'). But extract_patterns_v4.py was only reading 'reasoning' (the original WRONG prediction logic), ignoring 'reflection' entirely. This amplified signal inversion (K033): Sonnet read 'Greptile 5/5 → merge' (reasoning) instead of 'I over-trusted Greptile' (reflection). Fix: pass both fields with clear labels, instruct Sonnet to use reflection as primary source.
